title: Rumbold of Mechelen

infobox.value: [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") [Eastern Orthodox Church](/wiki/Eastern_Orthodox_Church "Eastern Orthodox Church")
[St Rumbold's Cathedral](/wiki/St_Rumbold%27s_Cathedral "St Rumbold's Cathedral") in Mechelen

Depicted as a [bishop](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") or a bearded man with a [hoe](/wiki/Hoe_(tool) "Hoe (tool)") lying under his feet. He may also be shown murdered near a [coffer of money](/wiki/Coffer_(furniture) "Coffer (furniture)").

(or *Rumold*, *Romuold*; [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Rombout*; [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Rombaut*; [Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *Rum(w)oldus, Rumwaldus, Rumboldus, Rumbaldus*; many variants overall) was an [Irish](/wiki/Irish_people "Irish people") or [Scottish](/wiki/Scottish_people "Scottish people") [Christian](/wiki/Christianism "Christianism") [missionary](/wiki/Missionary "Missionary"), although his true nationality is not known for certain.[[1]](#cite_note-tradition-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2004date1-2)[[3]](#cite_note-2004date2-3)
["Latin Saints of the Orthodox Patriarchate of Rome – 'R' *(for Rumoldus)*"](http://orthodoxengland.org.uk/saintsr.htm). Retrieved 5 Jul 2011.
Dries van den Akker s.j. (13 Sep 2010). ["Rombout van Mechelen"](http://www.heiligen.net/heiligen/06/24/06-24-0775-rombout.php) (in Dutch). www.heiligen.net. Retrieved 9 July 2011.
["Saint of the Day: 3 July - Index: Rumold(us)"](http://www.saintpatrickdc.org/ss/0703.shtml). St. Patrick Catholic Church, Washington, D.C, U.S.A. Retrieved 9 July 2011.

Mulder-Bakker, Anneke; Carasso-Kok, Marijke (1997). *Gouden legenden : heiligenlevens en heiligenverering in de Nederlanden*. Verloren. p. 145. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [9065502912](/wiki/Special:BookSources/9065502912 "Special:BookSources/9065502912").
T. Paaps: *Het leven van de heilige Gummarus*. In Dutch, originally from 1940, but rewritten in 2008 by Hugo Neefs and republished by "de Gilde Heren van Lier".

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Rumbold of Mechelen](/w/index.php?title=Rumbold_of_Mechelen&redirect=no "Rumbold of Mechelen"))

Irish or Scottish Christian missionary and martyr

| Saint Rumbold | |
| --- | --- |
| Saint Rumbold on a [chasuble](/wiki/Chasuble "Chasuble") in the [Basilica of Our Lady of Hanswijk](/wiki/Basilica_of_Our_Lady_of_Hanswijk "Basilica of Our Lady of Hanswijk"), Mechelen | |
| Born | possibly [Ireland](/wiki/Ireland "Ireland") or [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland") |
| Died | 6th, 7th or 8th century (see text) [Mechelen](/wiki/Mechelen "Mechelen") |
| Venerated in | [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") [Eastern Orthodox Church](/wiki/Eastern_Orthodox_Church "Eastern Orthodox Church") |
| Major [shrine](/wiki/Shrine "Shrine") | [St Rumbold's Cathedral](/wiki/St_Rumbold%27s_Cathedral "St Rumbold's Cathedral") in Mechelen |
| [Feast](/wiki/Calendar_of_saints "Calendar of saints") | 24 June |
| [Attributes](/wiki/Saint_symbolism "Saint symbolism") | Depicted as a [bishop](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") or a bearded man with a [hoe](/wiki/Hoe_(tool) "Hoe (tool)") lying under his feet. He may also be shown murdered near a [coffer of money](/wiki/Coffer_(furniture) "Coffer (furniture)"). |
| [Patronage](/wiki/Patron_saint "Patron saint") | [Mechelen](/wiki/Mechelen "Mechelen") and [Humbeek](/wiki/Humbeek "Humbeek") |

**Saint Rumbold** (or *Rumold*, *Romuold*; [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Rombout*; [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Rombaut*; [Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *Rum(w)oldus, Rumwaldus, Rumboldus, Rumbaldus*; many variants overall) was an [Irish](/wiki/Irish_people "Irish people") or [Scottish](/wiki/Scottish_people "Scottish people") [Christian](/wiki/Christianism "Christianism") [missionary](/wiki/Missionary "Missionary"), although his true nationality is not known for certain.[[1]](#cite_note-tradition-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2004date1-2)[[3]](#cite_note-2004date2-3)
He was [martyred](/wiki/Martyr "Martyr") near [Mechelen](/wiki/Mechelen "Mechelen") by two men, whom he had denounced for their evil ways.[[4]](#cite_note-CathOL-4)

Saint Rumbold's [feast day](/wiki/Feast_day "Feast day") is celebrated by the [Roman Catholic Church](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Church "Roman Catholic Church"), and [Western Rite Orthodox](/wiki/Western_Rite_Orthodoxy "Western Rite Orthodoxy") Churches, on 24 June;[[4]](#cite_note-CathOL-4)[[5]](#cite_note-OrthEU-5)
and it is celebrated in Ireland on 3 July.[[6]](#cite_note-DvdA-Romb-6)[[7]](#cite_note-spdc-7)
He is the [patron saint](/wiki/Patron_saint "Patron saint") of Mechelen,[[4]](#cite_note-CathOL-4) where [St. Rumbold's Cathedral](/wiki/St._Rumbold%27s_Cathedral "St. Rumbold's Cathedral") possesses an elaborate golden shrine on its high altar, containing [relics](/wiki/Relics "Relics") attributed to the saint. According to local lore, his remains are buried in the cathedral. Twenty-five paintings in the choir illustrate his life.

## Life and legend

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rumbold_of_Mechlin&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and legend")]

There are no contemporary historical records on the life of Rumbold and all stories about his life are legendary. According to legend, he was consecrated a regionary [bishop](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") at [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"). [Aodh Buidhe Mac an Bhaird](/wiki/Aodh_Buidhe_Mac_an_Bhaird "Aodh Buidhe Mac an Bhaird") (c. 1590–1635) argued that Rumbold was born in [Ireland](/wiki/Ireland "Ireland"). He is said to have been a [Bishop of Dublin](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Dublin "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Dublin"), the son of a [Scottish king](/wiki/List_of_Scottish_monarchs "List of Scottish monarchs"),[[4]](#cite_note-CathOL-4) and the brother of [Saint Himelin](/wiki/Himelin "Himelin").[[8]](#cite_note-8) He would have worked under [St. Willibrord](/wiki/Saint_Willibrord "Saint Willibrord") in the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands") and [Brabant](/wiki/Flemish_Brabant "Flemish Brabant"), and also have been a close companion of the [hermit](/wiki/Hermit "Hermit") [St. Gummarus](/wiki/Gummarus "Gummarus"),[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[1]](#cite_note-tradition-1)[[4]](#cite_note-CathOL-4)[[10]](#cite_note-10) and of the preacher monk Fredegand van Deurne, who, according to one tradition, maintained contact with [St. Foillan](/wiki/St._Foillan "St. Foillan") (who was murdered in the [Sonian Forest](/wiki/Sonian_Forest "Sonian Forest") around 665).[[11]](#cite_note-DvdA-Fred-11)

St. Rumbold's biography, written around 1100 AD by Theodoricus, prior of [Sint-Truiden](/wiki/Sint-Truiden "Sint-Truiden") Abbey, identified 775 as the year of the saint's death. The surrounding areas of Mechelen had in reality been Christianized much earlier.[[1]](#cite_note-tradition-1) In 2004 a state-of-the-art examination of the relics traditionally claimed to be St. Rumbold's showed a death date between 580 and 655.[[2]](#cite_note-2004date1-2)[[3]](#cite_note-2004date2-3)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## St. Rumwold of Buckingham

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rumbold_of_Mechlin&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: St. Rumwold of Buckingham")]

There has been some historical confusion between Rumbold of Mechelen and the infant Saint [Rumwold of Buckingham](/wiki/Rumwold_of_Buckingham "Rumwold of Buckingham"), who died in 662 AD at the age of 3 days. The latter is referred to as Romwold, Rumwald, Runwald, Rumbald, or Rumbold. A compilation of three saints' lives translated by Rosalind Love mentioned that in 15th-century records in [Salisbury](/wiki/Salisbury "Salisbury"), an unknown author 'corrected' the attribution as "martyr" (possibly the Rumbold murdered in Mechelen) by annotating "confessor" (as Rumwold was not a martyr). Also, the original dedication of churches to a St. Rumbold in Northern England appears uncertain.[[13]](#cite_note-TECALSL-excerpts-13)
